title: My Body Is Allowed to Want Space
subtitle: An affirmation for the touched-out feeling that carries guilt with it
excerpt: A short affirmation for when wanting physical space feels like failing at motherhood. The statement names the want and separates it from the love. Used when the need for space comes with guilt attached.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the need to be left alone in your own body comes with guilt. Good in the evenings or any moment when wanting space feels like a confession.

content.text: A one-minute affirmation for the want that comes with guilt. The want for space is real and biological. The guilt attaches itself to the wanting. The affirmation separates the two.
New to affirmations as a practice? Read
The affirmation names what is true and what the guilt conflates. It does not argue against the love. It separates the physical want from the emotional bond.
My body is allowed to want space. Wanting space is not the same as giving less love.
Say it when the guilt shows up alongside the need. Say it out loud if you can. Say it twice if the guilt is loud.
